# Paper 3 evidence-graph — narrative conflicts report

Disagreements the registry deliberately does NOT average away. Each is a surfaced config field / documented tension, not a merged constant.

## Gate-level conflict notes

| link | gate | conflict |
|---|---|---|
| `foster2025.infiltration::gate_infiltration_triangle` | `gate_infiltration_triangle` | Registry component tier is controlled_independent, but this gate's evidence is sign_or_compatibility on in-sample data (k from a kappa fitted to the same shot; front driven by that shot's own P(t); wide 1.86x porosity bracket). The 'independent' claim is not supported by the gate code. |
| `grudeva2025.reduced::gate_grudeva_no_eps_kappa` | `gate_grudeva_no_eps_kappa` | Encodes an intra/inter-source conflict: the adjudicated no-eps capacitance and kappa~2.2e-15 override S2's printed eps-multiplied equations and both sources' printed kappa=2.2e-16 / P_app=9.2e-6 (decade/exponent typos). |
| `mo2023_2.swelling::gate_mo2_fixed_flow_trends` | `gate_mo2_fixed_flow_trends` | Documents the divergence from cameron2020's non-monotonic fine-grind dip — the fixed-flow data are monotone (a fixed-flow machine defeats clogging), a noted contrast rather than a resolved conflict. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_intersource_spread` | `gate_g10_intersource_spread` | khomyakov2020's measured kinematic viscosity (converted via rho*nu) runs systematically ~+37% ABOVE the telisromero2001 Eq-10 closure across the Xw 76-90% / T 295-365 K overlap (median 25-50%, max <60%) — a bounded material/instrument offset (Russian production extract vs Brazilian 51-Brix batch) agreeing in sign but quantifying the G10 absolute-magnitude uncertainty at concentrated in-pore strengths. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_mu_bias_directional` | `gate_g10_mu_bias_directional` | Applies the ~1.3-2x envelope to the whole early shot, whereas gate_g10_telisromero_closure shows bulk shot-TDS mu is only ~1.06x water (the 1.3-2x belonging only to concentrated early in-pore liquor). |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_reference_mu_above_water` | `gate_g10_reference_mu_above_water` | The envelope estimate this gate checks (~1.3-1.9x water, 4-6e-4 Pa.s) conflicts with the Telis-Romero-2001 Eq-10 closure reconstruction (gate_g10_telisromero_closure), which puts BULK shot-TDS mu at only ~1.06x water and reassigns the ~1.3-2x magnitude to concentrated early in-pore liquor (Xw~76%), not bulk shot TDS. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_telisromero2000_thermal` | `gate_g10_telisromero2000_thermal` | Supplies the density used to convert local concentration to X_w in the mu(c,T) study (bulk rho ~1003, <=1% over water; first-drip rho ~1196); the viscosity magnitude tensions live in the companion 2001/bulk gates. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_telisromero_closure` | `gate_g10_telisromero_closure` | Its ~1.06x bulk-shot-TDS result corrects the paywalled-snippet envelope's ~1.3-2x guess that gate_g10_mu_bias_directional relies on, reassigning the 1.3-2x to concentrated early in-pore liquor only. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_telisromero_full_table` | `gate_g10_telisromero_full_table` | Anchors the MEASURED eta grid that yields ~1.06x-water bulk-shot viscosity, undercutting the paywalled-snippet envelope's ~1.3-2x guess; it does not reconcile the separate khomyakov2020 ~+37%-above-TR2001 offset. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_viscosity_bulk_negligible` | `gate_g10_viscosity_bulk_negligible` | Resolves the core G10 magnitude tension for BULK flow — measured TR2001 mu gives ~1.06x-water (negligible), refuting the ~1.3-2x snippet envelope for shot TDS — and is claimed robust even under the khomyakov2020 ~+37% offset. |
| `wadsworth2026.inertial::gate_inertial_de1_audit` | `gate_inertial_de1_audit` | Registry-internal source conflict: the wadsworth paper's own worked espresso band (Fo 0.0161-0.0639) vs the backlog's gusher-regime estimate (Fo ~0.3-0.9); the audit adjudicates toward the backlog's higher value. |
| `wadsworth2026.inertial::gate_inertial_fo_band` | `gate_inertial_fo_band` | The reproduced band 0.0161-0.0639 (laminar) conflicts with the registry backlog's Fo~0.3-0.9 gusher-regime estimate at Cameron bed density — an inter-estimate disagreement driven by different k/phi_p assumptions, adjudicated separately on DE1 traces (gate_inertial_de1_audit). |
| `wadsworth2026.inertial::gate_mo_reynolds_overlay` | `gate_mo_reynolds_overlay` | Encodes an inter-source convention conflict: mo2023's Re (SPH mu/rho, d43 length scale) and wadsworth's Fo_F are non-interchangeable diagnostics reported side by side; mo2023's k1/Forchheimer values also carry the §5.3 units caveat. |

## Un-reconciled published constants (surfaced, not merged)

### c_sat (saturation concentration, kg/m^3)
Per ROADMAP §5.4: 212.4 kg/m^3 is a single shared-provenance value across cameron2020/lee2023/moroney2016/egidi2024; grudeva's paper (224) and thesis (170) sets disagree with it and each other (grudeva card-of-record reconciliation is ROADMAP item 1.7a). The value is a surfaced config field, never a merged constant.

_Authoritative reconciliation: docs/ROADMAP.md §5.4._

| value | source(s) |
|---|---|
| 170 | `grudeva2023 (thesis)` |
| 212.4 | `cameron2020`, `lee2023`, `moroney2016`, `egidi2024` |
| 224 | `grudeva2026 (paper)` |
